name: Cinnamon and Sugar Candy Cane Twists
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour mixture with yeast from the foil packet and sugar. Stir in hot water, 2 tablespoons margarine, and egg until dough pulls away from sides of the bowl. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface. With greased or floured hands, shape dough into a ball. Knead dough for 5 minutes until smooth, sprinkling with additional flour if necessary to reduce stickiness. Cover with large bowl; let rest 5 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, in a small bowl, combine all filling ingredients except margarine. Mix until crumbly. Set aside.
3. On lightly floured surface, roll dough into a rectangle measuring 18x10 inches. Spread 2 tablespoons margarine lengthwise over half of dough; sprinkle with filling mixture.
4. Carefully fold untopped dough over filling; seal edges. With sharp knife, cut dough into 18 strips. Stretch each strip to 10 inches. Loosely twist and form into a candy cane shape. Place on greased cookie sheets. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and cloth towel. Let rise in warm place (temperatures between 90-100°F) for 3-5 minutes, or until doubled in size.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F. Uncover dough. Bake for 10-15 minutes, or until light golden brown. Remove from cookie sheets. Cool completely.
6. In a small bowl, blend glaze ingredients, adding enough milk for desired consistency. Drizzle over candy canes. Decorate with candies, if desired. Makes 18 rolls.
These delightful sweet rolls are simplified with hot roll mix. I have not tried this recipe. It comes from my Pillsbury "Holiday" cookbook #142. Marilyn Sultar
